titleStmt.title: SII 9.404: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 404. (A.R. No. 111 of 1913.) ON THE PEDESTAL OF A JAINA IMAGE KEPT IN THE TALUK OFFICE AT RAYADRUG, RAYADRUG TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.

summary.p: The record which is in Sanskrit verse is damaged and dated Śaka 1277, Manmatha, Mārgaśira, pūrṇimā, Bhṛigu[dina] corresponding to A.D. 1355 November 20 Friday. It refers itself to the reign of the Vijayanagara king Harihara and records that, by the command of the king, Bhōgarāja set up the god Ananta-Jinēśvara. His preceptor was Māghaṇandi, disciple of Amarakīrtti of the Nandi-saṅgha, Balātkāra-gaṇa, Sārasvata-gachchha and Kundakund-ānvaya.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīmanmanmathavatsarē girinagadvaṁdvēṁdu māsē mārggaśirābhidhē bhṛgu satpūrṇnamāyāṁ tithau . rājyaṁ śāsati rāyadurganagarē nāthaḥ svāṁgasamudbhavē hariharekṣmāpē
tadājñāvaśāt . śrīmūlasaṁghējani naṁdisaṁghastasminbalātkāragaṇōtiramyaḥ gacchētha sārasvatanāmadhēyē śrīkuṁdakuṁdānvayavārddhi caṁdraḥ . Ācāryyāmarakīrttisaṁyami
sutaḥ śrīmāghaṇaṁdivratī siddhāṁtābdhisudhākarō gururabhūdyasyaiṣa dākṣiṇyabhūḥ . māḷāyipriyarāmiseṭṭitanayaḥ śrībhōgarājō vyadhātpūtānaṁta
jinēśvarasya mahitaśrēṣṭhāṁ pratiṣṭhāṁ śriyai . śrī .
Digital edition of SII 9.404 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
